1	# Cycle 62 Round 1 Audit
2	
3	**Date:** 2026-06-20
4	**Status:** AUDIT / BANKABLE_LEMMA / COUNTERPACKET / ROUTE_CUT / CONDITIONAL
5	**Raw folder:** `experimental/notes/m1/cycle62_round1_raw/`
6	
7	## Executive Verdict
8	
9	This round is significant and route-changing.
10	
11	The scalar-apolar route survives at the algebraic reduction level but the
12	specific model local-limit theorem proposed for the next step is cut.
13	
14	The important distinction:
15	
16	- `L-LIST-APOLAR-ALL-LAYER-CI` appears to close as a bankable foundation.
17	- `L-LIST-MINIMAL-CI-GJ-FIBER` appears to close as the central algebraic
18	  reduction.
19	- The naive one-atom periodic quotient `Q_per` does **not** close the model
20	  local-limit wall. Role 04 gives an official-scale same-field counterpacket.
21	- The backup `t=1` MCA GJ-color route is corrected by thickening
22	  `Delta` to `Delta+[\beta]`.
23	- The projective equality-scale packet and degree-31 Lattes packet both pass
24	  as guard-lane audits.
25	- The right-factor-only two-block overlap route is false; common-left-composite
26	  / common-color closure is the correct repair.
27	
28	This is not a full solve. It is a successful proof/kill round that identifies
29	the next real obstruction.
30	
31	## Worker Verdicts
32	
33	### Role 01 - all-layer scalar apolar CI
34	
35	**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA
36	
37	The all-layer apolar complete-intersection foundation is asserted over every
38	finite field and every characteristic. The strongest useful formula is:
39	
40	```text
41	gcd(U_E,V_E) = product of locator factors for padded zero-coefficient
42	coordinates.
43	```
44	
45	Thus full-coordinate representations are exactly `gcd(U_E,V_E)=1`. This is
46	stronger than the prompt requested and should be banked after independent
47	human/PRZ review.
48	
49	### Role 02 - minimal CI generalized-Jacobian fiber
50	
51	**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA
52	
53	The central lemma `L-LIST-MINIMAL-CI-GJ-FIBER` is proved under the strict
54	hypothesis `j>d=sigma+1`. It gives:
55	
56	```text
57	N_s(sigma) =
58	1_{A squarefree and L-split} + N_{Delta,j}(b_Delta),
59	```
60	
61	with exact `G_Delta`, `G_eff`, and finite Fourier expansion.
62	
63	This is a genuine positive result. It closes the algebraic reduction targeted
64	by the Master Referee.
65	
66	### Role 03 - model GJ local limit
67	
68	**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA / CONDITIONAL
69	
70	Role 03 does not prove the all-field model local-limit theorem. It proves a
71	finite packet:
72	
73	- exact model boundary coordinates for `Delta=[0]+sigma[infinity]`;
74	- exact `G_eff` and Fourier formula;
75	- exact periodic-character charge;
76	- fixed-characteristic polynomial cap;
77	- norm-rigid finite local limit;
78	- a dyadic block lower floor;
79	- a finite-characteristic tensorized obstruction.
80	
81	The remaining wall is named:
82	
83	```text
84	W-LIST-MODEL-GJ-NEAR-SPLIT-COLLISION-CLASS-MASS
85	```
86	
87	This is useful, but Role 04 shows the declared `Q_per` formulation is too
88	weak.
89	
90	### Role 04 - model GJ counterpacket hunter
91	
92	**Label:** COUNTERPACKET
93	
94	This is the route-changing negative result.
95	
96	It gives an exact same-field, rate-`1/2`, `q<2^256` counterpacket in the
97	minimal scalar stratum:
98	
99	```text
100	q = 21*2^128 + 1,
101	n = 256,
102	k = 128,
103	sigma = 4,
104	j = 124,
105	deg A = 5,
106	deg B = 124.
107	```
108	
109	The packet has at least:
110	
111	```text
112	7,045,058,086,196,679
113	```
114	
115	full-coordinate listed words in one generalized-Jacobian fiber, while the
116	finite target is:
117	
118	```text
119	floor(q/2^128) = 21.
120	```
121	
122	The counterpacket does **not** invalidate the minimal CI/GJ fiber theorem. It
123	invalidates the next-step theorem that tried to charge coherent exceptional
124	mass using only one-atom periodic character pullbacks. The missing charge is
125	configuration-level block trade structure.
126	
127	The proposed repair wall is:
128	
129	```text
130	L-MODEL-GJ-MAXIMAL-BLOCK-TRADE-CHARGE
131	```
132	
133	Specifically, define and prove a canonical maximal `(K,D)` block-plus-defect
134	assignment and finite overlap theorem.
135	
136	### Role 05 - t=1 MCA GJ color
137	
138	**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA
139	
140	The `t=1` MCA backup route closes after a correction:
141	
142	```text
143	supports live in G_Delta,
144	colors live in G_{Delta+[beta]}.
145	```
146	
147	The old unthickened scalar-color claim is false; an explicit `F_11`
148	counterexample is supplied. The corrected MCA numerator is an occupied-lift
149	count in the thickened effective generalized Jacobian.
150	
151	Next wall:
152	
153	```text
154	W-JR-T1-GJ-PLUS-QUOTIENT-CONDITIONED-COLOR-OCCUPANCY
155	```
156	
157	### Role 06 - finite frontier checker
158	
159	**Label:** AUDIT
160	
161	The exact finite checker is specified and the repository has enough finite
162	definitions to implement it. It must keep separate:
163	
164	```text
165	q_gen, q_line, q_code, q_chal.
166	```
167	
168	It cannot mark anything safe from asymptotics or `n^{1+o(1)}`. This should be
169	implemented soon because Role 04 and the packet verifiers are explicitly
170	finite-threshold-sensitive.
171	
172	### Role 07 - projective equality-scale packet verifier
173	
174	**Label:** AUDIT
175	
176	The Cycle 60 projective equality-scale packet passes in the supplied audit
177	text/certificate:
178	
179	```text
180	28,048,800 > 16,776,960 = T_F.
181	```
182	
183	This confirms a guard-lane obstruction: fixed-coordinate monomial action ranks
184	do not detect the realized split-rational block system at `M=sigma`.
185	
186	### Role 08 - degree-31 Lattes verifier
187	
188	**Label:** AUDIT
189	
190	The degree-31 Lattes packet passes. It supplies a concrete field model,
191	isogeny data, fiber distribution, regularity information, and exact finite
192	comparison. This remains a guard-lane verification, not the main theorem.
193	
194	The degree-113 packet should remain unchecked unless the finite checker says
195	it matters at the first unresolved official reserve.
196	
197	### Role 09 - two-block overlap kill test
198	
199	**Label:** ROUTE_CUT
200	
201	The common-right-factor/refinement version of two-block overlap rigidity is
202	false. Even with coprime degrees and no common right factor, the block
203	incidence graph can have arbitrarily many components and exponentially many
204	common unions.
205	
206	The correct repair invariant is:
207	
208	```text
209	common left composite / common component color:
210	W = A o R = B o S.
211	```
212	
213	Next wall:
214	
215	```text
216	L-MCA-TWO-BLOCK-COMMON-COLOR-CLOSURE
217	```
218	
219	## Route Consequence
220	
221	The Master Referee plan was correct to move the main effort to scalar apolar
222	CI/GJ. Roles 01 and 02 validate that choice.
223	
224	However, the planned Role 03 local-limit theorem is not the right next theorem.
225	Role 04 cuts it. The next scalar wall is now configuration-level, not
226	one-atom-character-level:
227	
228	```text
229	L-MODEL-GJ-MAXIMAL-BLOCK-TRADE-CHARGE
230	```
231	
232	This should replace `W-LIST-MODEL-GJ-QUOTIENT-CONDITIONED-LOCAL-LIMIT` as the
233	central scalar-list Round 2 target.
234	
235	## Recommended Next Round
236	
237	Do **not** run another broad solve prompt.
238	
239	Round 2 should focus on the block-trade repair:
240	
241	1. Formalize the Role 04 counterpacket in repo language.
242	2. Prove the block-collapse lemma for `M>=sigma`.
243	3. Define canonical maximal `(K,D)` assignment.
244	4. Prove or kill finite overlap/non-double-counting for `(K,D)` profiles.
245	5. Connect this to Role 03's near-split collision-class-mass wall.
246	6. In parallel, implement the Role 06 finite checker skeleton.
247	7. In parallel, independently audit Role 01/02 proofs for hidden edge cases.
248	8. Preserve the Role 05 thickened-color correction for later MCA transfer.
249	9. Treat Lattes/projective verifications as confirmed guardrails unless the
250	   finite checker says they alter the first unresolved official reserve.
251	
252	## Next Exact Lemma
253	
254	Primary:
255	
256	```text
257	L-MODEL-GJ-MAXIMAL-BLOCK-TRADE-CHARGE
258	```
259	
260	Statement to test:
261	
262	For `Delta=[0]+sigma[infinity]`, cyclic `H`, and each subgroup `K<=H` of order
263	`M>=sigma`, define
264	
265	```text
266	beta_K(cK)=prod_{u in K} alpha_Delta(cu).
267	```
268	
269	Prove the block-collapse identity:
270	
271	```text
272	beta_K(cK) has no jet coordinates of degrees 1,...,sigma-1
273	and depends only on c^M.
274	```
275	
276	Then define a canonical, shear-invariant maximal `(K,D)` assignment for support
277	profiles
278	
279	```text
280	D union union_{cK in S} cK,
281	```
282	
283	and prove a finite overlap/non-double-counting theorem. If this fails, the
284	scalar apolar route needs another structural invariant.
285	
286	Secondary:
287	
288	```text
289	W-LIST-MODEL-GJ-NEAR-SPLIT-COLLISION-CLASS-MASS
290	```
291	
292	for the finite-characteristic residual outside the norm-rigid and
293	cyclotomic-rank-safe ranges.
294	
295	## Director Call
296	
297	This was a good round. The route did not "solve"; it sharpened.
298	
299	The algebraic spine is now stronger than before. The next obstruction is also
300	sharper: coherent exceptional mass comes from block/configuration trades, not
301	from individual periodic characters.
302	
303	Round 2 should be designed around that fact.
304	
305

# Cycle 62 Round 1 Audit

**Date:** 2026-06-20
**Status:** AUDIT / BANKABLE_LEMMA / COUNTERPACKET / ROUTE_CUT / CONDITIONAL
**Raw folder:** `experimental/notes/m1/cycle62_round1_raw/`

## Executive Verdict

This round is significant and route-changing.

The scalar-apolar route survives at the algebraic reduction level but the
specific model local-limit theorem proposed for the next step is cut.

The important distinction:

- `L-LIST-APOLAR-ALL-LAYER-CI` appears to close as a bankable foundation.
- `L-LIST-MINIMAL-CI-GJ-FIBER` appears to close as the central algebraic
  reduction.
- The naive one-atom periodic quotient `Q_per` does **not** close the model
  local-limit wall. Role 04 gives an official-scale same-field counterpacket.
- The backup `t=1` MCA GJ-color route is corrected by thickening
  `Delta` to `Delta+[\beta]`.
- The projective equality-scale packet and degree-31 Lattes packet both pass
  as guard-lane audits.
- The right-factor-only two-block overlap route is false; common-left-composite
  / common-color closure is the correct repair.

This is not a full solve. It is a successful proof/kill round that identifies
the next real obstruction.

## Worker Verdicts

### Role 01 - all-layer scalar apolar CI

**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA

The all-layer apolar complete-intersection foundation is asserted over every
finite field and every characteristic. The strongest useful formula is:

```text
gcd(U_E,V_E) = product of locator factors for padded zero-coefficient
coordinates.
```

Thus full-coordinate representations are exactly `gcd(U_E,V_E)=1`. This is
stronger than the prompt requested and should be banked after independent
human/PRZ review.

### Role 02 - minimal CI generalized-Jacobian fiber

**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA

The central lemma `L-LIST-MINIMAL-CI-GJ-FIBER` is proved under the strict
hypothesis `j>d=sigma+1`. It gives:

```text
N_s(sigma) =
1_{A squarefree and L-split} + N_{Delta,j}(b_Delta),
```

with exact `G_Delta`, `G_eff`, and finite Fourier expansion.

This is a genuine positive result. It closes the algebraic reduction targeted
by the Master Referee.

### Role 03 - model GJ local limit

**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA / CONDITIONAL

Role 03 does not prove the all-field model local-limit theorem. It proves a
finite packet:

- exact model boundary coordinates for `Delta=[0]+sigma[infinity]`;
- exact `G_eff` and Fourier formula;
- exact periodic-character charge;
- fixed-characteristic polynomial cap;
- norm-rigid finite local limit;
- a dyadic block lower floor;
- a finite-characteristic tensorized obstruction.

The remaining wall is named:

```text
W-LIST-MODEL-GJ-NEAR-SPLIT-COLLISION-CLASS-MASS
```

This is useful, but Role 04 shows the declared `Q_per` formulation is too
weak.

### Role 04 - model GJ counterpacket hunter

**Label:** COUNTERPACKET

This is the route-changing negative result.

It gives an exact same-field, rate-`1/2`, `q<2^256` counterpacket in the
minimal scalar stratum:

```text
q = 21*2^128 + 1,
n = 256,
k = 128,
sigma = 4,
j = 124,
deg A = 5,
deg B = 124.
```

The packet has at least:

```text
7,045,058,086,196,679
```

full-coordinate listed words in one generalized-Jacobian fiber, while the
finite target is:

```text
floor(q/2^128) = 21.
```

The counterpacket does **not** invalidate the minimal CI/GJ fiber theorem. It
invalidates the next-step theorem that tried to charge coherent exceptional
mass using only one-atom periodic character pullbacks. The missing charge is
configuration-level block trade structure.

The proposed repair wall is:

```text
L-MODEL-GJ-MAXIMAL-BLOCK-TRADE-CHARGE
```

Specifically, define and prove a canonical maximal `(K,D)` block-plus-defect
assignment and finite overlap theorem.

### Role 05 - t=1 MCA GJ color

**Label:** BANKABLE_LEMMA

The `t=1` MCA backup route closes after a correction:

```text
supports live in G_Delta,
colors live in G_{Delta+[beta]}.
```

The old unthickened scalar-color claim is false; an explicit `F_11`
counterexample is supplied. The corrected MCA numerator is an occupied-lift
count in the thickened effective generalized Jacobian.

Next wall:

```text
W-JR-T1-GJ-PLUS-QUOTIENT-CONDITIONED-COLOR-OCCUPANCY
```

### Role 06 - finite frontier checker

**Label:** AUDIT

The exact finite checker is specified and the repository has enough finite
definitions to implement it. It must keep separate:

```text
q_gen, q_line, q_code, q_chal.
```

It cannot mark anything safe from asymptotics or `n^{1+o(1)}`. This should be
implemented soon because Role 04 and the packet verifiers are explicitly
finite-threshold-sensitive.

### Role 07 - projective equality-scale packet verifier

**Label:** AUDIT

The Cycle 60 projective equality-scale packet passes in the supplied audit
text/certificate:

```text
28,048,800 > 16,776,960 = T_F.
```

This confirms a guard-lane obstruction: fixed-coordinate monomial action ranks
do not detect the realized split-rational block system at `M=sigma`.

### Role 08 - degree-31 Lattes verifier

**Label:** AUDIT

The degree-31 Lattes packet passes. It supplies a concrete field model,
isogeny data, fiber distribution, regularity information, and exact finite
comparison. This remains a guard-lane verification, not the main theorem.

The degree-113 packet should remain unchecked unless the finite checker says
it matters at the first unresolved official reserve.

### Role 09 - two-block overlap kill test

**Label:** ROUTE_CUT

The common-right-factor/refinement version of two-block overlap rigidity is
false. Even with coprime degrees and no common right factor, the block
incidence graph can have arbitrarily many components and exponentially many
common unions.

The correct repair invariant is:

```text
common left composite / common component color:
W = A o R = B o S.
```

Next wall:

```text
L-MCA-TWO-BLOCK-COMMON-COLOR-CLOSURE
```

## Route Consequence

The Master Referee plan was correct to move the main effort to scalar apolar
CI/GJ. Roles 01 and 02 validate that choice.

However, the planned Role 03 local-limit theorem is not the right next theorem.
Role 04 cuts it. The next scalar wall is now configuration-level, not
one-atom-character-level:

```text
L-MODEL-GJ-MAXIMAL-BLOCK-TRADE-CHARGE
```

This should replace `W-LIST-MODEL-GJ-QUOTIENT-CONDITIONED-LOCAL-LIMIT` as the
central scalar-list Round 2 target.

## Recommended Next Round

Do **not** run another broad solve prompt.

Round 2 should focus on the block-trade repair:

1. Formalize the Role 04 counterpacket in repo language.
2. Prove the block-collapse lemma for `M>=sigma`.
3. Define canonical maximal `(K,D)` assignment.
4. Prove or kill finite overlap/non-double-counting for `(K,D)` profiles.
5. Connect this to Role 03's near-split collision-class-mass wall.
6. In parallel, implement the Role 06 finite checker skeleton.
7. In parallel, independently audit Role 01/02 proofs for hidden edge cases.
8. Preserve the Role 05 thickened-color correction for later MCA transfer.
9. Treat Lattes/projective verifications as confirmed guardrails unless the
   finite checker says they alter the first unresolved official reserve.

## Next Exact Lemma

Primary:

```text
L-MODEL-GJ-MAXIMAL-BLOCK-TRADE-CHARGE
```

Statement to test:

For `Delta=[0]+sigma[infinity]`, cyclic `H`, and each subgroup `K<=H` of order
`M>=sigma`, define

```text
beta_K(cK)=prod_{u in K} alpha_Delta(cu).
```

Prove the block-collapse identity:

```text
beta_K(cK) has no jet coordinates of degrees 1,...,sigma-1
and depends only on c^M.
```

Then define a canonical, shear-invariant maximal `(K,D)` assignment for support
profiles

```text
D union union_{cK in S} cK,
```

and prove a finite overlap/non-double-counting theorem. If this fails, the
scalar apolar route needs another structural invariant.

Secondary:

```text
W-LIST-MODEL-GJ-NEAR-SPLIT-COLLISION-CLASS-MASS
```

for the finite-characteristic residual outside the norm-rigid and
cyclotomic-rank-safe ranges.

## Director Call

This was a good round. The route did not "solve"; it sharpened.

The algebraic spine is now stronger than before. The next obstruction is also
sharper: coherent exceptional mass comes from block/configuration trades, not
from individual periodic characters.

Round 2 should be designed around that fact.

content.thinking:
I'm reviewing the current state of the problem—checking the route board, active walls, and banked lemmas to confirm where things stand, then looking at the director state file. The core setup involves a cyclic group $H$ (like $H_0 = \mu_{256}$ in $F_{17^{16}}$) with some parameter $\Delta$, though the thought cuts off before fully stating the mathematical question.

The boundary map $\Phi_\sigma$ encodes a subset $T$ as its product along with the first $\sigma-1$ elementary symmetric functions, and I'm trying to bound the size of fibers—the number of weight-$j$ subsets that map to the same boundary value. The full-block trade theory shows that adding or removing a complete coset of a subgroup $K$ (with $|K| \ge \sigma$) preserves the elementary symmetric coordinates while only changing the product, so these "full blocks" can be traded within a fiber without affecting the jet coordinates. Role 05 introduces a counterexample showing that the block-free supports alone can be enormous—at least $2^{32}$ in a single fiber—which undermines hopes for a tight bound based on blocking structure.

The counterpacket uses partial pattern gadgets: 48 states in the 16th roots of unity with vanishing first two elementary symmetric functions, lifted via squaring into a cyclic subgroup of order 32 so that the lifted set has elementary symmetric polynomial $\equiv 1 \pmod{z^6}$, and then constructs supports as unions of 7 oriented lifted blocks plus a marker, yielding over 52 billion distinct supports in one fiber. The real question now is whether I can extend the charge mechanism from full blocks to local prefix-collision equivalence classes—where two gadgets are equivalent if they have the same size and their elementary symmetric polynomials agree modulo $z^\sigma$—and whether this enlarged framework can partition the fibers without double-counting while giving an explicit total charge.

The key move is that swapping gadgets $A \to B$ within their support preserves the global elementary symmetric polynomial modulo $z^\sigma$, hence preserves the jet part of the boundary, while the product color shifts by the ratio of their product exponents, tracked by an enumerator. For the Role 05 packet specifically, each of the 7 oriented blocks is a size-16 gadget supported on a single coset with elementary symmetric polynomial $\equiv 1 \pmod{z^6}$, and their product colors are determined by the exponents from the underlying base sets.

Now I'm working out how many such gadgets exist within a fixed coset and what product colors they can have. The lifted states give 48 total states that scale into the coset, and as I vary the scaling parameter $a$ over $\mathbb Z/16$, the product exponent cycles through only two values since $8a \bmod 16$ is either 0 or 8. The gadget enumerator for a single coset is $W = 8(Y^1 + Y^4 + Y^7 + Y^9 + Y^{12} + Y^{15})$ in product-color notation. The key question is whether this gadget formalism actually provides a bound tighter than the fiber size itself, or if it's just a relabeling—this gets at the heart of what the referee was questioning about whether canonical naming alone is sufficient without a compatible charge structure.

The full-block charge works as a genuine reduction because it decomposes the fiber into block-bearing and block-free parts, where the block-bearing supports are organized by defect and coset choices with product conditions, giving a structured count rather than a tautological one. But Role 05 demonstrates that the block-free residual is itself enormous, which means the reduction isn't achieving the target bound.

The gadget strategy is to charge these block-free supports by identifying local configurations on smaller subgroups that are equivalent modulo the constraint, then trading each gadget for its equivalence-class partners. For this to actually reduce the fiber count rather than just re-describe it, I need three things: a canonical way to locate gadgets in each support, non-overlapping coverage, and the total charge summing to something genuinely smaller than the naive fiber size. In Role 05, every support decomposes into exactly seven gadgets plus a marker, where each gadget is a size-16 configuration on an oriented coset with the constraint satisfied, so the natural gadget class at the subgroup scale is well-defined.

The key insight is that the product-color enumerator of each gadget class has a specific structure — supported on a particular coset of residues — and when I multiply these enumerators across all seven cosets and extract the coefficient matching the global product condition, I recover exactly the fiber count of 52,747,567,104. This means the gadget decomposition isn't just a reinterpretation; it's capturing the fiber structure perfectly because the packet was constructed precisely as a product of independent gadget choices subject to one global constraint.

However, the charge equals the count itself, which means while this is a valid partition (no double-counting), it doesn't actually bound the count downward — the referee's warning holds. The real test for the prize is whether summing the charges across all gadget profiles stays within the expected bound, and Role 05 already demonstrates it doesn't: the packet alone exceeds 2^32, which violates the bound.

So the gadget formalism succeeds at organizing the packet into a single charge object for partition purposes, but that charge isn't bounded by the baseline term — the gadget approach doesn't close the gap unless there's additional cancellation or a constraint I'm overlooking.

Let me reconsider whether the gadget charge is truly forced to equal the full packet count. Could a maximal gadget assignment with non-double-counting be more clever than just taking the product of per-coset enumerators? The key insight is that the gadget partition directly partitions the supports by profile, and the charge per profile is an exact count of that subset. So the total charge must equal the fiber count exactly — no partition can ever make the count smaller than itself. The bound has to come from somewhere else: comparing the fiber count to the baseline through equidistribution or character theory.

This means the gadget partition is really just a bookkeeping tool, not a bound itself. The actual bound comes from analyzing the coefficient extraction — showing that the enumerators are spread out enough that the coefficient at any single target is small relative to the total. The fiber size should be around the baseline by a standard character argument over the group, but the gadget structure reveals that the boundary map is far from equidistributed on these special block-free supports. The gadgets create a low-entropy, collision-prone subset, which is why the deviation can be so large.

Now I'm checking whether this matters at the finite scale. Looking at the Role 05 packet with its enormous field size, the baseline ratio is tiny, but the packet itself is around $5.27 \times 10^{10}$. So the fiber size is vastly larger than what the equidistribution hope would predict — a deviation factor of at least $5 \times 10^{10}$ at the official reserve. This is a genuine, finite-scale failure, not just a theoretical concern. The only way this could be prize-irrelevant is if the finite target threshold is itself at least that large.

But wait — if the code field is $F_{17^{16}} \approx 2^{65.4}$, then the target $T_{\text{code}} = \lfloor 2^{65.4}/2^{128}\rfloor = 0$. A list size of $5 \times 10^{10}$ versus a target of zero would be a catastrophic failure of list-decodability, which would actually disprove the claim rather than support it. I think I'm tangling myself up here. Let me refocus on what the project is actually trying to establish.

The "RS-MCA Proximity Prize" is fundamentally about proving or disproving a list-decoding and minimum-distance bound. The finite reserve $T_{\text{code}}$ acts as the threshold — any packet exceeding it becomes a counterpacket to that reserve. The gadget wall, though, is narrower in scope: it's a model-level question about whether the generalized-Jacobian boundary organizes and constrains the model fiber. Whether that feeds into the prize theorem is something the finite checker would determine.

I need to step back and decide on the right verdict here. Let me quickly check the route board and active walls for exact labels and constraints, then formulate my answer.

The honest assessment is nuanced. I can prove that the prefix-collision gadget partition exists exactly and without double-counting if I define gadgets via a canonical scale-and-cell assignment — this generalizes the full-block case and is genuinely bankable progress showing the bookkeeping is consistent. But the gadget charge itself doesn't yield a bound below the fiber count. For the Role 05 packet, the canonical maximal charge equals exactly the fiber cardinality, which exceeds the baseline bound by a factor that grows without limit. So the partition-and-charge is correct bookkeeping but fails as a residual bound.

The real obstruction shifts to bounding the per-class gadget enumerator coefficient — a new, sharper wall. The partition-and-charge theorem is provable and absorbs Role 05 as one charged class, but since the charge equals the count exactly, it can never bound the count below itself. A genuine bound would require a spread or equidistribution estimate on the gadget enumerators instead.

So I'm settling on **BANKABLE_LEMMA** as the label: I can bank the exact gadget partition and charge as a real generalization of the full-block theory that absorbs Role 05, while being crystal clear that this is bookkeeping (an exact formula, not a useful bound) and explicitly stating the true remaining obstruction — the gadget-enumerator spread bound. But I should double-check whether there's any genuine non-trivial inequality the gadget theory actually provides beyond just the count itself.

The real tension is whether the gadget partition is truly non-overlapping or if there's a hidden ambiguity like the $2^s$ aliases that plagued full blocks. The canonical assignment needs to fix a unique decomposition, and while full blocks were resolved by saturating at the least scale then maximizing, gadgets are trickier because many different size-16 subsets of a coset can satisfy the prefix condition. The natural approach is to decompose by scale and cell occupancy rather than by the specific gadget choice, but I need to verify whether a single coset's occupancy can be decomposed in multiple ways.

The cleaner structure emerges when I think about it multiplicatively: the global $E_T$ factors as a product over cosets, and modulo $z^\sigma$, each coset either contributes trivially (if $E_{T\cap C} \equiv 1$) or contributes nontrivially. Calling a coset "neutral" when it contributes trivially and "charged" otherwise, the jet part of the boundary depends only on the charged cells, leaving the neutral cells free to vary.

For Role 05 specifically, at scale $J = K$ with 8 cosets in $\mu_{256}$: the marker coset is the sole charged cell (carrying the $-z$ term), while the 7 oriented cosets are all neutral with size 16 and trivial $\sigma$-prefix, so they're free to be gadget-traded without affecting the boundary jet.

The charge count works out to $c_7(4) \cdot 8^7$, which gives roughly $5.27 \times 10^{10}$ — consistent with the neutral-cell mass of $48^7$ reduced by the color constraint. Now I need to verify there's no double-counting across scales: a neutral cell at scale $K$ shouldn't decompose and get charged again at a finer scale like $M_0$, so I need to pin down the canonical scale rule carefully. The cleanest approach is to fix a single scale $J$ and work with that partition: the fiber count factors as a convolution of neutral-cell color enumerators, with no double-counting since it's a single-scale decomposition. The cross-scale maximal assignment using laminarity is a harder layer I can flag separately. The real new wall is bounding the convolution coefficient itself—I need to define the neutral-cell color enumerator and express the fiber charge for a given charged pattern as a product over the neutral cells.

But here's the critical discovery: Role 05 shows that even a single charged marker cell with 7 neutral cells already violates the baseline, and the raw neutral class size at those parameters is far too large. This means the pure counting bound is fundamentally broken—the gadget route fails just like the block route did. The only way forward is to invoke constraints that live outside the model fiber count: the minimal-CI conditions, full-coordinate restrictions, or actual code-field constraints, or perhaps a rank cap that I haven't yet identified.

Since Role 05 §3.7 shows the cyclotomic-rank cap doesn't help and §3.8 confirms the packet sits in the minimal-CI stratum, my best remaining hope is that the finite checker will reveal this field isn't actually the official frontier. That's why the referee pushed for running it first. I'm now confident in the verdict, so I'll write it up starting with the bankable lemma—the exact neutral-cell partition-and-charge theorem that generalizes block collapse and absorbs Role 05 as a single charged pattern with explicit enumerator convolution. The partition is exact and non-double-counting at fixed scale, and Role 05 is absorbed cleanly, but the charge equation itself still can't bound below the model fiber count without those external constraints. I'm verifying the arithmetic that underpins Role 05 — checking that the partition cardinality and the charge formula both compute correctly — before committing to the lemma statement.

# Route Board

Labels are literal: PROOF, COUNTERPACKET, BANKABLE_LEMMA, ROUTE_CUT, EXACT_NEW_WALL.

## Cycle 42 Restricted Good-Reduction / Split-Density Closure And Reserve-Lift Wall

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE42_EXTERNAL_5P5PRO_GOODRED_DENSITY_AUDIT.md`

Audit judgment:

- Four external 5.5 Pro answers plus returned checker/certificate artifacts
  agree that Cycle 41's A-side raw affine/Cramer `G2/G3` failure was a false
  negative, not a source-valid obstruction.
- The correct view is that the raw Cycle 41 gate was sufficient for one
  simple affine chart but not necessary for tame/projective good reduction.
- The fixed restricted branch

```text
q_gen = p,
B = F_p,
F = F_{p^2},
q_line = p^2,
D = F_p,
t = sigma = 2,
j = 4
```

  has external-machine certificates for Subcase A at `p=7` and Subcase B at
  `p=19`/`p=31`, with geometric `S_4` witnesses.
- The resulting restricted split-slope density is

```text
N_split(p) = p^2/24 + O(p^(3/2))
           = q_line/24 + O(q_line^(3/4)).
```

- Codex syntax-checked and source-inspected the returned checkers but did not
  locally rerun the SymPy computations because `sympy` is absent and no
  dependency was fetched.
- This is a restricted local closure only. It is not a corrected-reserve,
  fixed-rate, generated-field, MCA/list/line-decoding/curve-MCA, protocol,
  SNARK, prize, or final `COUNTERPACKET` statement.

Current exact wall:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-STRUCTURED-COSUPPORT-RESERVE-LIFT
```

The next target is a homerun/reserve-lift attempt: either lift the closed
fixed quartic mechanism to growing reserve `t=sigma >= C n/log n`,
`j=Theta(n)` with source-valid separated denominator/cosupport data and
positive noncontained split-slope density, or prove a theorem-sized
obstruction showing why the quartic branch cannot scale.

## Cycle 40 Subcase Finite-Place Geometric S4 And Char0Delta Wall

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT /
answered by Cycle 42 and superseded by
`W-F1-AA-RES-STRUCTURED-COSUPPORT-RESERVE-LIFT`.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE40_SUBCASE_GOODRED_AUDIT.md`
- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/local_checks/20260618_cycle40_subcase_goodred_checker_result.json`

Audit judgment:

- Cycle 40 banks the finite-place histogram criterion:

```text
off-singular factorization types "4" and "13"
  => G_arith = G_geom = S_4
```

- Codex extracted and ran the parametrized checker locally.
- The checker reports `all_pass=true`:

```text
Subcase A, ell=alpha: p=7,23,43,47 pass.
Subcase B, ell=-2X:  p=11,19,31,59 pass.
```

- This closes the Subcase A finite-place gap, but it does not prove
  characteristic-zero good reduction or a growing-prime theorem.
- This is not a `COUNTERPACKET`, corrected-reserve theorem, MCA, CA, list,
  line-decoding, curve-MCA, protocol, SNARK, or prize statement.

Current exact wall:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-CHAR0DELTA
```

The next target is a homerun/full-solve attempt at the characteristic-zero
branch determinant, quartic discriminant, good-reduction prime, and tame
specialization bridge per subcase.

## Cycle 39 Locator Collapse And Subcase Good-Reduction Wall

Status: PROOF / BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT / answered by
Cycle 40.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE39_SYMBOLIC_GOODRED_AUDIT.md`
- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/local_checks/20260618_cycle39_locator_collapse_verify_result.json`

Audit judgment:

- Cycle 39 proves the restricted locator-collapse lemma for the explicit
  `t=2,j=4` family:

```text
ell=[X^p-X]_E = alpha   if (-5/p)=+1,
ell=[X^p-X]_E = -2X     if (-5/p)=-1.
```

- Thus the surface family is fixed within two congruence subcases, rather
  than varying freely with `p`.
- Cycle 38's `p=31` finite-place certificate belongs to Subcase B only.
- Subcase A and good reduction remain unproved.
- This is not a uniform theorem and not a `COUNTERPACKET`.

Current exact wall:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-GOODRED-SUBCASE
```

The next target is a subcase-separated `S_4` / good-reduction certificate, or
a precise obstruction showing that one subcase cannot globalize.

## Cycle 38 Repaired Finite-Place S4 Certificate And Symbolic Good-Reduction Wall

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT / answered by
Cycle 39.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE38_HOMERUN_S4_REPAIR_AUDIT.md`
- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/local_checks/20260618_cycle38_single_prime_s4_cert_patched_checker.py`
- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/local_checks/20260618_cycle38_single_prime_s4_cert_patched_stdout.txt`

Audit judgment:

- Cycle 38 identifies the exact Cycle 37 checker bug: four top free
  coefficients were residue-pairs where the checker needed `F`-elements.
- The patched data are
  `W_{n-1..n-4}=1,alpha,1+alpha,1`, with `u=[W]_E=1+X` and `kappa=1`.
- Codex ran the patched checker locally at `p=31`; it reports
  `PASS_S4_finite_place=true`, with factorization types `"4"` and `"13"`,
  a nonsquare-discriminant witness, and no singular points on the tested line.
- This is finite-place `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence and a bankable checker
  repair. It is not a uniform theorem and not a `COUNTERPACKET`.

Former exact wall:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-S4-SYMBOLIC-GOODRED
```

Cycle 39 answers this by proving locator collapse and sharpening the wall to
the subcase good-reduction certificate.

## Cycle 36 Explicit A2_B Family And Single-Prime S4 Wall

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT / answered at
finite-place level by Cycle 38 and superseded by symbolic good reduction.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE36_A2B_UNIFORM_S4_AUDIT.md`

Audit judgment:

- Cycle 36 banks an explicit source-valid denominator family for primes
  `p = 3 mod 4`:

```text
B=F_p, F=F_{p^2}=B(alpha), alpha^2=-1,
E=X^2+alpha X+1, b=X, D=F_p.
```

- For `a in F_p`, `E(a)=(a^2+1)+alpha*a`, so `E` has no `F_p` root.
  Also `E-E^tau=2 alpha X` and `E(0)=1`, so the denominator is separated.
- The remaining free data are finite nonvanishing choices, notably
  `kappa != 0`.
- Uniform geometric S4 is reduced to a finite single-good-prime certificate
  plus good-reduction monotonicity.
- Correction: resolvent irreducibility plus nonsquare discriminant is not
  enough by itself. The certificate must also prove quartic
  transitivity/geometric irreducibility, for example by a finite-place
  factorization type `"4"`.

Current exact wall:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-SINGLE-PRIME-S4-CERT
```

For the explicit family, produce or specify a reproducible good-prime
certificate checking determinant/top-symbol gates, quartic transitivity,
resolvent irreducibility, and discriminant nonsquareness. Success would still
be only a restricted local `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket seed, not a
corrected-reserve, MCA, line-decoding, protocol, SNARK, or prize theorem.

## Cycle 35 A2_B Finite-Place S4 Certificate And Uniform-S4 Wall

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT /
answered by Cycle 36 and superseded by the single-prime certificate wall.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE35_A2B_GEOMETRIC_S4_AUDIT.md`

Audit judgment:

- Cycle 35 banks a finite-place monodromy certificate for a fixed
  source-valid restricted `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` instance.
- If the off-`Delta` squarefree specializations of `L_tau` over `F_p` contain
  factorization types `"4"` and `"13"`, then arithmetic monodromy is `S_4`.
- Since `"13"` is an even degree-one Frobenius type, the sign constant-field
  obstruction is absent in that certified instance; hence
  `G_geom=G_arith=S_4`.
- The Cycle 32 `p=29` histogram contains `"4"`, `"13"`, and `"1111"`, so it is
  strong `EXPERIMENTAL / AUDIT` evidence for one tested instance.
- This is not a uniform-in-`p` theorem and not yet a `COUNTERPACKET`.

Current exact wall:

```text
W-F1-AA-RES-T2J4-A2B-UNIFORM-S4
```

Construct an explicit source-valid growing-prime family and prove geometric
`S_4` plus arithmetic/geometric equality uniformly, or find the exact
constant-field/resolvent/source-validity obstruction. A genuine uniform proof
would give a restricted local `Theta(q_line)` counterpacket seed; it would
still not be corrected-reserve, MCA, line-decoding, protocol, SNARK, or prize
status.

## Cycle 34 A2_B Dominance And Geometric-S4 Wall

Status: BANKABLE_LEMMA / ROUTE_CUT / EXACT_NEW_WALL / AUDIT / superseded by
Cycle 35 finite-place certificate.

Source:

- `current_loop_20260618/2026-06-18-fable-loop/audits/20260618_CYCLE34_A2B_DOMINANCE_RESOLVENT_AUDIT.md`

Audit judgment:

- Cycle 34 banks the restricted dominance lemma for the off-curve
  `t=2,j=4` `A^2_B` family.
- On the source-valid dense open, the rational map
  `psi:z |-> tau(z)=M(z)^(-1)(-C_0(z))` has generic `B`-Jacobian rank two.

  `q_gen=p`, `t=sigma=2`, `j=4`, off-`R0`, source-valid separated window,
  the distinct split-quartic gate can be written in co-support coordinates
  `tau=e(T)` as one `F`-quadric, equivalently two `B`-quadrics:

```text
Phi(tau)=iota wedge_F mu = 0,
iota = u*lambda - ell*[Q_S]_E,
mu = b*lambda,
lambda=[L_T]_E.
```

```text

  = kappa*N_{A/F}(lambda)

    + tau_4*(kappa*Tr_{A/F}(lambda') - (ell[Q_S]_E) wedge_F b)
    + (kappa*N_{A/F}(lambda') - (ell[Q_S]_E) wedge_F (b lambda')).
```

content.thinking:
Now I'm working through the mathematical setup carefully. I have a cyclic subgroup of the multiplicative group of a finite field, a divisor on the projective line, and I'm about to define the model boundary map that connects these pieces together.

The map $\Phi_\sigma$ takes a subset $T$ and outputs both the product of its elements and the elementary symmetric polynomials up to degree $\sigma - 1$, which can be read off from the generating function $E_T(z)$ truncated modulo $z^\sigma$. The key question is counting how many $j$-subsets of $H$ map to a given boundary value $b$ under this map.

I'm now reviewing the key structural results: full blocks of size at least $\sigma$ become invisible to the jet part of the map since their generating function is congruent to 1 modulo $z^\sigma$, while they contribute a fixed product value. There's also a canonical assignment procedure that organizes supports by their block structure and defect.

The exact formula decomposes the fiber count into a block-free term (supports avoiding any full eligible block) plus a correction sum over defects with empty core. However, the block-free term alone can be astronomically large—over $2^{32}$ in concrete examples—and this grows multiplicatively across tensor products. So charging only full blocks isn't enough to control the count, and the proposed fix involves prefix-collision gadgets that charge more refined collision patterns beyond just full blocks.

Now I'm working through the Role 05 construction as a concrete test case: a field extension $\mathbb{F}_{17^{16}}$ with a 256th root of unity, a subgroup $K$ of order 32 inside $H_0$, and eight cosets of $K$ within $H_0$, where one coset (the identity coset) is reserved for special handling.

content.text:
EXACT_NEW_WALL

I read the Cycle 63 R2 audit, Role 05 (the char-17 counterpacket), Role 09 (referee board), Roles 03/04/02 (the banked block layer), and the Cycle 62 audit. The verdict below proves the structural half of the target wall, kills its quantitative half, and states the exact surviving obstruction with a concrete finite falsifier.

---

## 1. Executive verdict and confidence

The target wall `L-MODEL-GJ-PREFIX-COLLISION-GADGET-PARTITION-AND-CHARGE` splits cleanly into three pieces, and the honest outcome is mixed:

- **(Bankable, 0.98).** There is an *exact* prefix-collision gadget partition: for any divisor subgroup `J<=H`, the model fiber `Phi_sigma^{-1}(b)` is enumerated, with no overlap, as a single group-ring convolution coefficient of per-coset gadget enumerators. The gadget classes are genuine atoms. This is a real new structural lemma.
- **(Route-cut, 0.97).** Precisely *because* that partition is exact and overlap-free, the charge it produces **equals** `N_Delta(b)`. Role 05 shows `N_Delta(b_0) >= 52,747,567,104 > 2^32`, tensoring to `|P_0|^R`. So the gadget charge is provably **not** a scalar-list smallness certificate. No "canonical / maximal / bounded-overlap" refinement can help: overlap is already exactly zero and the count is still exponential. The hope encoded in the wall name (gadgets bound the block-free residual) is dead at the scalar-support-mass level.
- **(New wall, 0.9).** The Role 05 packet **is** absorbed — as a product of exactly 7 gadget-class enumerators plus a marker — but absorption is descriptive, not a bound. The prize-relevant quantity is not the total gadget mass but the **number of occupied thickened MCA colors**, i.e. the cardinality of the *support* of the pushed enumerator. That is the exact surviving wall, and I give a finite falsifier for it.

Confidence that the scalar-support route is cut as a smallness mechanism: high. Confidence that the packet is finite-*relevant* to an official reserve: low — `n=256, sigma=6` is a toy/sub-reserve model instance, so the live decision is for the frontier checker.

---

## 2. Formal statements

Throughout, `H<=F^x` is finite cyclic, `Delta=[0]+sigma[infty]`, and for `T subseteq H`,
```
E_T(z) = prod_{x in T}(1 - x z),    pi(T) = prod_{x in T} x.
```
Write `G_sigma = (1 + z F[z]/(z^sigma))^x` (the jet group; `|G_sigma| = |F|^{sigma-1}`) and `Pi = H` (product-color group). Because `E_T(z)` has constant term 1 it lands in `G_sigma`; the boundary is
```
Phi_sigma(T) = ( pi(T) ; E_T(z) mod z^sigma )  in  Pi x G_sigma,
```
with the product coordinate `pi(T)=e_{|T|}(T)` kept **separate** from the jet `(e_1,...,e_{sigma-1})` (these are independent since `|T|=j >> sigma`).

### Definition (local prefix-collision gadget)
Fix `J<=H`. A *gadget* is specified by: ground set = one `J`-coset `C` (multi-coset variant in §3.6); subgroup scale `J`; prefix modulus `sigma`; size `m`; product-color group `Pi`. Two subsets `A,B subseteq C` collide,
```
A ==_sigma B   iff   |A|=|B|=m   and   E_A(z) == E_B(z) (mod z^sigma).
```
A gadget class `Cc` is one `==_sigma` class. Its **product-color enumerator** is
```
W_Cc(Y) = sum_{A in Cc} Y^{pi(A)}   in   Z[Pi],
```
and its common jet is `g_Cc = E_A(z) mod z^sigma` (well-defined on the class). Multiplicity = the integer coefficients of `W_Cc`.

### Theorem A — exact gadget convolution (bankable: `L-MODEL-GJ-PREFIX-COLLISION-GADGET-CONVOLUTION`)
Let `H = ⊔_{C in H/J} C`. Define the per-coset partition element in `Z[q] ⊗ Z[G_sigma x Pi]`:
```
Z_C(q) = sum_{A subseteq C} q^{|A|} [E_A mod z^sigma] [pi(A)]
       = sum_{m>=0} q^m sum_{Cc subseteq C, |Cc|=m} [g_Cc] · W_Cc(Y).
```
Then
```
prod_{C in H/J} Z_C(q) = sum_{T subseteq H} q^{|T|} [E_T mod z^sigma] [pi(T)],
```
and therefore, for `b = (p_b ; g_b)`,
```
N_Delta(b) = #{ T in C(H,j) : Phi_sigma(T)=b }
           = [ q^j · g_b · p_b ]  prod_{C in H/J} Z_C(q).      (★)
```
The right side is an **exact** finite count; the gadget enumerators `W_Cc` are its only non-trivial inputs. For a *fixed* `J` the decomposition is a genuine set partition, so there is **zero overlap** (goal 2 holds exactly).

### Theorem B — absorption of the Role 05 packet (verification)
Take `J = K = <eta^8>`, `|K|=32`, `|H/K|=8`. With the reserved coset holding only `{1}` and each of the 7 cosets `eta^t K` holding one of the 48 lifted states `eta^t Ã` (all of size 16, all jet-trivial `E == 1 mod z^6`), the packet is the *restricted* slice of (★):
```
|P_0| = [ q^113 · (1-z) · Y^0 ]  ( q[1-z]Y^0 ) · prod_{t=1}^{7} ( q^{16}[1] W_t(Y) )
      = [Y^0] prod_{t=1}^{7} W_t(Y),     W_t(Y) = 8·Y^{t}·(Y^1+Y^4+Y^7+Y^9+Y^{12}+Y^{15}).
```
Hence `|P_0| = 8^7 · [Y^{-28}] (sum_{u in S} Y^u)^7 = 8^7 · c_7(4) = 2,097,152 · 25,152 = 52,747,567,104`, reproducing Role 05 exactly. **The packet is absorbed as exactly 7 charged gadget classes plus one marker.** (Goal 4: absorbed — *descriptively*.)

### Theorem C — the gadget charge is not a smallness certificate (route-cut)
Any "total charge" derived from a fixed-`J` gadget partition is, by (★), identically `N_Delta(b)`. Since
```
N_Delta(b_0) >= |P_0| = 52,747,567,104 > 2^32,   and   N_Delta(b_R) >= |P_0|^R,
```
no gadget charge can bound `N_Delta(b)` below any block-scale, polynomial, or `2^{O(n)}`-with-small-constant target. The block-free residual is genuinely exponential, and the gadget formalism re-expresses it without shrinking it. (Goals 1–3 hold *exactly* but *vacuously* as a bound.)

### Theorem D — the sharper wall (`W-MODEL-GJ-THICKENED-GADGET-COLOR-OCCUPANCY`)
Let `Delta^+ = Delta + [beta]`, `beta in F^x \ H`. The thickening map is
```
rho_beta(T) = [ L_T(beta) ] = prod_{x in T}(beta - x)  in  F^x / (scalar),
```
a coordinate on the fiber `G_{Delta^+} -> G_Delta` (fiber group `~= F^x`). The **MCA occupancy** over `b` is
```
Occ(b) = #{ occupied thickened colors } = | supp_{Pi x F^x} ( push of (★) by rho_beta ) |,
```
the number of **distinct** colors hit, *not* the total mass. The prize-relevant inequality is `Occ(b) <= T_line = floor(q_line / 2^128)` at the first unresolved official reserve. The gadget enumerator `W_Cc` is the exact object to push: occupancy = number of monomials surviving in `prod_t ( push of W_t )` after fixing the scalar boundary.

---

## 3. Proofs and edge cases
